data.title: Add .editorconfig template for cross-editor consistency
data.description:
## Summary

Deploy `.editorconfig` template from `dotfiles-templates/` to provide language-specific coding style rules that work across all editorconfig-aware editors.

## Context

The `dotfiles-templates/.editorconfig` provides:

* UTF-8 encoding, LF line endings, trailing whitespace removal
* Language-specific indentation rules:
  * YAML/JSON/JS: 2 spaces
  * Python: 4 spaces (PEP 8)
  * Shell scripts: 2 spaces
  * Go/Makefiles: tabs (as required)
* Works automatically in vim, neovim, vscode, zed, emacs, sublime, intellij, etc.

## Current State

* ✅ Source template exists: `dotfiles-templates/.editorconfig`
* ❌ Not deployed by system-setup ansible roles
* ❌ Users must configure editor-specific settings manually

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Copy `dotfiles-templates/.editorconfig` to `system-setup/roles/03-dotfiles/files/.editorconfig`
- [ ] Add deployment task in `roles/03-dotfiles/tasks/main.yml`:

  ```yaml
  - name: Symlink editorconfig for users
    ansible.builtin.file:
      src: "{{ role_path }}/files/.editorconfig"
      dest: "/home/{{ item.name }}/.editorconfig"
      state: link
      force: true
      owner: "{{ item.name }}"
      group: "{{ item.name }}"
    loop: "{{ users }}"
    become: true
  ```
- [ ] Test deployment with `./run.sh`
- [ ] Verify editorconfig is respected in vim/neovim/zed

## Implementation Notes

* Deploy to `~/.editorconfig` (home directory) for global effect
* Users can override with project-specific `.editorconfig` files (editorconfig searches upward)
* File should be symlinked (not templated) unless user-specific rules needed
* Consider templating if different users need different default indent sizes

## Priority

**High** - Cross-editor consistency with minimal configuration

## References

* Source: `/home/skogix/skogix/dotfiles-templates/.editorconfig`
* Target role: `system-setup/roles/03-dotfiles/`
* EditorConfig spec: [https://editorconfig.org/](<https://editorconfig.org/>)
* Related: Part of dotfiles-templates integration initiative
